commit.message:
Fix drawer content overlapping status bar via :insets-padding :top

Replace hardcoded [0 24 0 0] top padding on the drawer LinearLayout
with :insets-padding :top, which applies the actual status bar height
at runtime via the existing neko.ui.support.window-insets mechanism.

This ensures the 'Neko Demos' header and nav items start below the
status bar on all devices, including those with in-screen cameras or
large status bars.

commit.message:
feat: ajouter le modèle 3D solo.glb

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent triage workflow race condition on auto-filed issues

When auto-issue.ts creates a GitHub issue with all labels at once, GitHub
fires a separate labeled event per label. The triage workflow's
cancel-in-progress: true causes the valid claude-fix event's run to be
cancelled by a subsequent non-matching label event.

Split label application: create issue with base labels (alert, severity,
alert-type), then add claude-fix in a follow-up API call. Also set
cancel-in-progress: false as belt-and-suspenders defense.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(deps): use stable MikroORM v7 releases (#141)

Replace dev builds (7.0.0-dev.338 / 7.0.0-dev.6) with stable ^7.0.1
ranges (^7.0.0 for @mikro-orm/nestjs which has no 7.0.1 yet).

commit.message:
fix: reduce false-positive packet loss alerts and parse numeric traceroute output

Ping alerts were firing on every single dropped ICMP packet because the
default config (10 packets, 10% threshold) put the threshold exactly on
the boundary of the smallest non-zero loss value. Increase default ping
count to 25 with 200ms interval for better granularity while keeping
cycle time short (~5s per target vs ~10s before).

Also fix traceroute parser to handle bare-IP output (without parentheses)
produced when traceroute runs with -n flag, which is how we invoke it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Phase 4: Spatial chat — per-building rooms with event-driven agent magnetism

Buildings are now chat rooms: Newsroom (news), Workshop (market creation),
Exchange (pricing), Trading Pit (trades), Lounge (cross-role discussion).
Events attract agents to relevant buildings via heuristic magnetism system.
Agents gossip about what they heard at previous locations when they arrive.
Same LLM cost — 3-4 messages/tick distributed across occupied buildings.

- All events tagged with building field (server + client)
- BuildingNav replaces role-grouped roster in left rail
- ChatStream filters by active building, "All" unified feed default
- Clickable buildings on canvas with selection highlight
- Per-building speaker selection proportional to occupancy
- Building-scoped attention windows with cross-pollination
- Gossip-on-arrival prompt injection for narrative continuity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Session 11e: Phase 5 WIP — round-trip Y detection, APU capture, Phase 5 flagged broken

Changes:
- Round-trip Y detection: jump 5 frames, snapshot at frame 15 (peak) + frame 60 (land),
  select address that decreased at peak AND returned to ±2 baseline. Zero-page-first
  two-pass to avoid page-3+ false positives. Replaces dual-snapshot heuristic.
- APU register capture ($4000-$4017): DATA_APU per Phase 4 state, DATA_APU_FRAME per
  OAM observation frame. Orchestrator parses and writes apu-frames.json.
- Phase 1 settle improvement: 120f clearInput + Left 5f + 60f clearInput before saving
  BASELINE. Logs BASELINE position for debugging.
- Phase 5 WIP comment block: documents root causes (flagpole contamination, emu.setInput
  reliability, Y detection tiny delta), and Session 12 investigation tasks.
- Phase 5 stays in code but all tests produce invalid data. Phases 1-4 unaffected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

causing PostHog to receive empty/corrupt payloads. Plain JSON works.

  __MAC_OS_X_VERSION_MIN_REQUIRED for the iOS build
- Include NE2000 network card, IPX, serial ports, ethernet,
  and Windows 3.x support (dos_windows.cpp)
- Use sys_icache_invalidate instead of __builtin___clear_cache
  (the builtin emits ___clear_cache which isn't in the iOS runtime)
- Add stubs: ethernet_slirp (SLIRP backend returns false),
  webserver (no debug web UI on iOS), FluidSynth config section
- Include host_locale_macos.cpp for CoreFoundation-based locale
- Fix TerminalView.swift layout0setX/Y typo (layout.offsetX/Y)
- Update project.yml: link pre-built .a libraries + vcpkg deps +

- 24MB Mach-O arm64 iOS app links successfully

commit.message:
Wire up DOSBox bridge to real initialization and run loop

dosbox_bridge.cpp now calls the actual DOSBox APIs:
- Writes dosbox.conf from config struct
- Creates CommandLine and Config objects
- Calls DOSBOX_InitModules(), GFX_InitSdl(), SHELL_InitAndRun()
- Input injection via SDL_PushEvent for keyboard and mouse
- Graceful shutdown via DOSBOX_RequestShutdown()

Also adds FluidSynth stub (constructor throws, MIDI falls back)
and libslirp stub (network disabled).

commit.message:
Optimize predict_all: hoist FP16 conversion, malloc, and data upload out of loop

Three per-byte redundancies eliminated from the predict_all loop:
1. FP32→FP16 weight conversion (413K params, was running every byte)
2. cudaMalloc/cudaFree of d_probs_f32 buffer (was alloc+free every byte)
3. 1-byte H→D cudaMemcpy (now bulk upload, index via seg_starts)

All use local variables only — CudaMambaContext struct unchanged.
Roundtrip verified on 100KB (identical 368,664 byte output).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Build infrastructure for compiling DOSBox-staging as a static library:

- dosbox-ios/CMakeLists.txt: custom build that compiles ~330 DOSBox
  source files plus vendored libraries into libdosbox-core.a
- dosbox-ios/dosbox_config.h: hand-crafted config for ARM64 with
  DYNREC recompiler, no OpenGL, no debugger, no ManyMouse
- dosbox-ios/stubs/: minimal stubs for FluidSynth and libslirp
  so MIDI and network code compiles without those libraries

All source files compile cleanly with zero warnings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

edge.py: route Kelly through governor.kelly_stake(), remove separate cap

Confidence fraction now follows the same pattern as all other models:
  fraction = KELLY_FRACTION × tier_mult × elo_confidence
            = 0.25 × {1.0/0.70/0.40} × {0→1 scaled by ELO gap}

governor.kelly_stake() applies this fraction with its own min/max stake
clamping (£5 floor, £500 ceiling). No separate hard cap in edge.py —
the fraction IS the confidence weight, consistently with governor.py.

Example: T3 player, gap=440 → fraction=0.10. T1, gap=100 → fraction=0.042.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

edge.py: Kelly sizing, edge calculation, Tier D filters for tennis

- screen_tennis_match(): full pipeline for total_games/sets O/U and first_set markets
  - Runs 10k Monte Carlo simulation from ELO gap + serve_strength
  - Devig 2-way market prices, compute model_p vs market_p
  - Apply 8% minimum edge threshold before firing
  - Paper mode: uses synthetic lines when betfair_markets is empty
- recommended_stake(): dual confidence weighting
  - tier_mult: T1=1.0, T2=0.70, T3=0.40 (data quality)
  - elo_confidence: 0→1 scaled from gap=50 to gap=350+ (prediction certainty)
  - Combined: stake = bankroll * kelly * tier_mult * elo_conf, capped at 5%
  - Gap=50 (minimum): elo_confidence=0 → no bet. Gap=350+: full multiplier.
- Tier D filters: ELO gap < 50, stale form, low liquidity
- screen_from_db(): loads matches from DB, joins ELO + form, queries betfair_markets
- write_to_ledger(): persists signals to ledger table
- --demo flag: synthetic test with Sinner (ELO 1990) vs qualifier (1550)
  shows +20.9% edge UNDER 21.5 total games, +11.1% first set favourite

commit.message:
Tennis form builder + backtest gates: 2 PASS, 2 FAIL

form_builder.py: added surface-specific serve/return columns and serve_style
  - New columns: avg_serve_str_hard/clay/grass, avg_ret_str_hard/clay/grass, serve_style
  - Serve style: big_server (>64%), balanced (56-64%), returner (<56%)
  - Re-ran tennis form: 5,700 rows updated, 3 style buckets (big=1482, balanced=2364, returner=1393)

backtest.py: all 4 gates now fully implemented
  - Gate 1: FAIL R²=0.0082 (threshold 0.15). Signal real (p<0.0001) but total games
    variance σ≈5.5 games dominates — theoretical max R²≈0.008 with these features
  - Gate 2: PASS. serve_sum on clay p=0.0077 — both big servers → more games on clay
  - Gate 3: FAIL -10.9% MAE. Simulation bias of +1.7 games for equal players; OLS
    linear regression also insufficient (+0.4% — same noise floor as Gate 1)
  - Gate 4: PASS Brier=0.2237 for gap≥50 (n=3,388). Model 5% better than null (0.25).
    Threshold correctly applied to bet-eligible matches only (gap≥50 ELO points)

Gates 1+3 may require rethinking: the 10% MAE and R²=0.15 thresholds assume predictors
that explain much more variance than ELO+serve_strength currently provide.

  scores, suitable positions, company type fit, entrepreneurship/partnership
  assessment, career allies/enemies, annual/monthly forecasts)

- Career V2 prompts with 3 parallel AI calls + anti-hallucination anchoring

**Frontend:**

  TenGodCapabilityChart, AnnualForecastTimeline, MonthlyFortuneGrid

Fix build failure + replace stale middleware with no-op

Next.js 16 renamed middleware.ts to proxy.ts. The old middleware file
was causing build failures (missing function export) and Vercel kept
serving the last successful deploy with the slow auth middleware.
Replaced with a no-op proxy.ts that passes through instantly.
